weatherReport:
This weekend incredibly productive, I was so busy on Sunday I'm having to submit this on a Monday. 
First, I designed and constructed the absolutely massive neon development purpose white paper that covers all the information about how and why the system was built, what it can do, it's purpose, why it's important, the crucible year, the weekly roundup and work intake portal, everything.  
I also created a new phase analysis file that's 10 times better, deeper, richer, for thorough analysis than full_phase_a1_a5 and better structured or organised for machine Reading .  I also got a lot of painting done and starting to have some solid progress in the studio.  And on Sunday I had dinner with the editor or Tatler magazine for Hong Kong and they loved hearing about neon and the whole project and we are gonna figure out how to display it for art month Art Basel in Hong Kong.

thingResisted: Can't think of anything really that stands out

weatherReport: This week went really well.  I ordered a bunch of new larger brushes that got delivered and also forged some into a 30cm brush that's been so much fun to play with.   Worked on a dozen new large paintings , slowly starting to make solid progress in the right direction.   I completely rebuilt the website gallery and made it much more user friendly and visually much much better.   And above all that, I completely rebuilt and updated the entire neon file system and made it a "masterpiece" that's about as advanced as It can physically be with current systems.  I rebuilt the entire system , every core file, and it is now operating at many levels higher than it was before.  This week I got a lot done.
worksMade: Will submit the crucible intake logs with this update.

weatherReport: This week was quite good.  My mom has come to visit form Canada and will be here for a month (not staying in the studio) so it's been a very good and grounding time to spend with her, yet still being very productive.  This week I brought the entire neon system to as close to a presentation level as has ever been.  The white paper has been fully filled out and caught up, formatted and stabilised.  I've sent it off as well as the introduction media to Tatler magazine and lets hope for  good for art Basel.  The theory of what I'm doing is starting to catch up with my conscious mind from my subconscious, the bone, flesh and breath and the consequence of permanence are all starting to bare weight and my practice, very good.  I painted a lot this week, started a lot of new works.  It's hard to make the roundup and the intake logs sync up because I'll usually photograph and upload them in batches of "completed" works so the dates and times might not exactly sync up.  Last week's roundup and intake records caused a bit of chaos in the system when I discussed working on lots of pieces but it east reflected in the data, but that was purely down to this reason, the roundups and the intake portal will harmonize over the course of time , but week to week there may be statistical anomalies, which is fine. Sciatica was a bit worse this week again but I chalk that up to increased movement and stress with showing my mother around town. My work is finally starting to show promise, this week was the first time in the crucible year I can see the light at the end of the tunnel as far as creating work goes, a lot of that comes down to the new broad brushes I ordered and the very large brushes I crafted together.   This was the first time I've shared a batch of images over Instagram of the works in progress and received solid feedback.  I was expecting the process to take longer before hitting my stride but seems I'm ahead of schedule, which is very optimistic.

thingWorked: Painting, the big brushes  are producing work in genuinely happy with with the direction of.  Band the theory and philosophy behind it is coming together.

weatherReport: This week I didn't get much studio work done as I brought my mother up to chiang mai, my first time here also.  Early week I did get a bunch of painting done, but I'm currently in Chiang mai so I can't upload the intake portal of works, I'll add them to next week's round up.  Finally got Claude pro and opus with extended thinking absolutely crushed a bunch of neon work.   I completely rebuilt the entire database and made it's at least 2 - 3 times more efficient and significantly more sophisticated, it's now operating at the level Id hoped it could, I'm really very happy with it.   Got a lot of sightseeing done, went to the top of Thailand's highest mountain today , spent a lot of food quality time with my mom which has been really important. Have been just focusing on doing neon work while you here. Also built the full visual databse files so it can match the writing to the actually image for reference now for the first time.   Completed the full cannonical biography timeline for my life, filling in significant gaps in the archives history. And much more.

weatherReport: This week I spent with my mom touring the south of Thailand, spent a few days on Koh Phi Phi and just arrived at Koh lanta, so once again I can't update the crucible intake form because I'm out of the studio, so don't take the lack of updated work cause any negative thoughts.  I've taken the opportunity though to do significant work on neon and have fully implemented the Kaiser Wilhelm update.   A fully system restructure that eliminated all dead Links, ghost files and phantom directions.   The system is now operating at zero latency, fully interconnected and damn near flawless, it it shows it the reasoning and conversation since.  I'm testing to see if the quick notes get updated into this roundup or not, it might be the last one on the legacy system.   I fully updated the weekly roundup page and it's completely modernised as well.  There were significant breakthroughs in theory and core system understanding too that happened this week, which will either the attached as quick notes or they won't, either way it's all good the quick note system will be there for next week regardless

weatherReport:
Preface: I'm still testing the quick notes feature.  There will either be none, 2 specifically from this week, or all of them, if last week's are included too just disregard those. 

This week was the end of my mom's trip here so it was mostly spent in Koh Phi Phi and Koh lanta.  I found there that going for motorcycle rides has the same walking engine effect and I went for a lot of long rides.  I got back to the studio very excited and have been painting non stop.  An important and crucial note is that all the painting added to the intake portal were the ones made during previous week's that I wasn't able to update while out of town. So this week will seem like an anomaly and needs to be properly referenced to avoid confusion.    I had an amazing call with my friend Victoria lo, who has been a big supporter of mine,she sponsored the hotel I painted thr3e and covenant in and Ioana very influential person in the Hong Kong and global art world.  She absolutely loves the direction of my new works and is extremely impressive neon and i am hoping will come on board to help take it to the next level. I'm also right in the middle of a visual media roll out to share the crucible works for the first time and getting pretty positive feedback.  Other than being at the beach and the crushing it once i got back to he studio I can't think much else my Brain's a bit fried hahaa.  Sciatica is a bitch again but I'm handling it fine
worksMade: Things are really moving along well now
walkingInsights: It surfaced that motorbike rides have the same mental effect
